articles.title: Occidental Mindoro town forecasts heat index of 45°C
articles.description: CALAPAN CITY, Oriental Mindoro — The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRMO) of San Jose, Occidental Mindoro, warned on Monday that the town’s heat index could reach 45°C, as Calapan City hits an “extreme caution” reading of 40°C. The City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Department (CDRRMD) said the 11 a.m. heat index was recorded at the Calapan City Public Market monitoring station on Monday. articles.title: ‘Absolutely MASSIVE 20 INCH rats’ are invading homes in one of UK’s historic counties & experts warn you could be next

articles.title: Cool Baguio feels the heat: Index nears record at 28°C on May 14
articles.description: BAGUIO CITY — While much of Northern Luzon continues to experience dangerous heat index levels reaching up to 46 degrees Celsius, even the typically cool Baguio City neared its historical peak, with a 28 degrees Celsius on May 14. Weekly data from the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) released on Monday covering May 13 to 17, showed Tuguegarao City again recording the highest heat index in Northern Luzon at 46°C on May 15. The same level was also logged on May 12 and May 14. READ: HEAT INDEX WATCH: Danger levels expected in Metro Manila, 36 others [...]... articles.title: ITCZ to bring rains over Southern Mindanao on Monday – Pagasa
articles.description: MANILA, Philippines — The Intertropical Convergence Zone (ITCZ) is expected to bring cloudy skies and scattered rains over southern Mindanao on Monday, May 18, according to the state weather bureau. The ITCZ, according to the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa), refers to the cloud clusters that make up a line where winds from the northern and southern hemispheres meet. READ: Agriculture officials fear ‘super’ El Niño In its morning weather forecast, Pagasa weather specialist Aldczar Aurelio said the ITCZ will cause scattered rains and thunderstorms over the following areas: “We expect this ITCZ to continue its impact [...]... articles.title: Severe storms possible Sunday night near Kansas City; Monday could be worse
articles.description: Strong to severe storms are possible Sunday evening and overnight in the Kansas City area. The Storm Prediction Center, part of the National Weather Service, has placed parts of far northeast Kansas and northwest Missouri at an enhanced risk (Level 3 of 5) of severe weather. Meanwhile, areas just north of Kansas City are at a slight risk (Level 2 of 5), while the metro area is at a marginal risk (Level 1 of 5).

articles.title: Longwood Gardens: A jewel of nature
articles.description: After industrialist Pierre S. du Pont bought a small farm outside Philadelphia to save its trees from being sold for lumber, he spent the rest of his life creating his dream oasis. Today, the 1,700-acre Longwood Gardens, recently expanded, continues its mission of providing "joy and inspiration to everyone through the beauty of nature, conservation, and learning." Jim Axelrod reports.
